questTitle: O-Level Physics: Density, Forces, Pressure & Motion Worksheet
subject: Physics
description: Master density calculations, pressure concepts (P=F/A), mass-weight relationships, force-motion relationships, and fundamental mechanics through scenario-based questions. All force problems are one-dimensional. Aligned with Singapore O-Level Physics standards. Show clear working for calculations.

questions.question: A materials scientist compares two cubes of identical size but made from different substances. One cube feels significantly heavier when lifted. Which physical property explains this difference in heaviness per unit volume?
A laboratory technician measures a solid metal block. It has a mass of 450 g and a measured volume of 50 cm³. What is the density of the metal?
Object A has a density of 0.8 g/cm³ and Object B has a density of 1.2 g/cm³. Both are gently released into a tank of water (density 1.0 g/cm³). Which outcome occurs?
An astronaut brings a calibration mass from Earth to a lunar base where the gravitational field strength is much lower. How do the mass and weight of the object change upon arrival?
options: Mass remains unchanged while weight decreases
Mass decreases while weight remains unchanged
questions.answer: Mass remains unchanged while weight decreases

questions.question: A shipping container has a mass of 15 kg. Taking the gravitational field strength as 9.8 N/kg, what is the gravitational force pulling it downwards?
Which statement correctly defines pressure in physics?
options: Force per unit area acting perpendicular to a surface
Weight divided by gravitational field strength
questions.answer: Force per unit area acting perpendicular to a surface

questions.question: A rectangular block exerts a force of 120 N on a horizontal surface. The contact area is 0.03 m². What pressure does the block exert on the surface?

questions.problemSet: CONCEPT CHECK: Pressure from Multiple Tyres
questions.question: A car rests on four identical tyres. The pressure exerted by the ground on one tyre is measured to be 20 kPa. What is the total pressure exerted by the ground on all four tyres combined?

A solid cube of uniform material exerts a pressure P on a horizontal surface. If an identical cube is made from the same material but with each side length tripled, what pressure does the larger cube exert on the surface?
A person wearing stiletto heels stands on soft ground. The same person wearing flat shoes stands on the same ground. Why do the stiletto heels sink deeper?
options: The person's weight is greater when wearing heels
The contact area is smaller, resulting in greater pressure for the same force
The ground becomes softer under the heels
The force exerted by the heels is larger
questions.answer: The contact area is smaller, resulting in greater pressure for the same force

questions.question: A spacecraft coasts through deep space with all thrusters switched off. It continues moving in a straight line at a constant speed indefinitely. What explains this sustained motion?
options: Its velocity naturally increases without force
Space exerts a forward pushing force
No resultant external force is acting on it
Its engines are still producing minimal thrust
questions.answer: No resultant external force is acting on it

questions.question: Engineers test two identical conveyor carts on a straight track. Cart X is subjected to a net horizontal force exactly three times the magnitude of the net force applied to Cart Y. How does Cart X's acceleration compare to Cart Y's?
options: It is the same as Cart Y's
It is one-third as large as Cart Y's
It is three times as large as Cart Y's
It is nine times as large as Cart Y's
questions.answer: It is three times as large as Cart Y's

questions.question: A 9 kg laboratory trolley is pushed along a frictionless track by a net horizontal force of 36 N. What is the magnitude of its acceleration?
When a sprinter pushes forcefully backward against their starting blocks at the beginning of a race, they accelerate forward. Which physical interaction is responsible for the forward acceleration?
options: The sprinter's momentum transfers directly to the ground
Gravity pulls the sprinter forward along the track
The blocks exert an equal forward force on the sprinter's feet
Air resistance pushes the sprinter in the direction of motion
questions.answer: The blocks exert an equal forward force on the sprinter's feet

questions.question: A worker pulls a 5 kg crate horizontally to the right with a force of 40 N. Friction opposes the motion with a force of 15 N to the left. What is the crate's acceleration?
An elevator car with a total mass of 600 kg accelerates vertically upwards at 2.0 m/s². Taking g = 10 m/s², what is the tension in the supporting steel cable during this upward acceleration?
A parachutist jumps from an aircraft. After falling for a while, their downward speed stops increasing and remains constant. What physical condition has been reached at this stage?
options: Upward air resistance has become zero
Upward air resistance equals downward weight, giving zero resultant force
Downward weight exceeds upward air resistance, causing constant acceleration
Upward air resistance exceeds downward weight, causing upward acceleration
questions.answer: Upward air resistance equals downward weight, giving zero resultant force

questions.question: In a vacuum chamber, a 5 kg steel ball and a 50 g feather are dropped simultaneously from the same height. With air resistance eliminated, what observation matches the expected outcome?
options: Both accelerate at the same rate but the heavier object lands first
The feather accelerates faster due to lower inertia
The steel ball accelerates faster and lands first due to greater weight
Both accelerate at the same rate and reach the floor simultaneously
questions.answer: Both accelerate at the same rate and reach the floor simultaneously

questions.question: A scientific probe weighs 80 N on Earth (where g = 10 N/kg). It lands on Mars, where the gravitational field strength is 4 N/kg. What is the probe's mass, and what is its weight on Mars?

A textbook rests motionless on a horizontal table. The Earth pulls the book downwards (weight), and the table pushes the book upwards (normal force). Despite being equal and opposite, why are these two forces NOT considered an action-reaction pair?
options: They act in the same direction
They are not equal in magnitude
They both act on the same object (the book)
questions.answer: They both act on the same object (the book)

questions.question: A student wants to measure the pressure exerted by a rectangular block on a table. Which measurements are essential to calculate this pressure?
options: Mass of block and height of block only
Weight of block and area of contact surface only
Volume of block and density of material only
Length, width, and height of block only
questions.answer: Weight of block and area of contact surface only

questions.question: A brick exerts a pressure of 2500 Pa on the ground when resting on its largest face. If the same brick is turned to rest on its smallest face, which has one-fifth the area of the largest face, what pressure does it now exert?
A group is determining the density of a small metal cube. Which of the following equipment changes would DECREASE the accuracy of their final result?
options: Repeating the mass measurement three times and averaging
Using a digital balance with 0.01 g precision instead of 1 g
Replacing a standard ruler with vernier calipers
Using a plastic ruler marked only in centimetres instead of millimetres
questions.answer: Using a plastic ruler marked only in centimetres instead of millimetres

questions.question: A model rocket has a total mass of 2.0 kg. At launch, its engine produces an upward thrust of 30 N. Taking g = 10 m/s² and ignoring air resistance, what is the rocket's initial upward acceleration?

A wooden crate of mass m is pulled horizontally across a rough floor by an applied force F. A constant frictional force f opposes the motion. Which algebraic expression correctly gives the crate's horizontal acceleration a?
